Kxf6 is the right move to learn here. Your king takes the undefended white queen on f6, so you win a big piece rather than leaving it sitting there. After moving to f6, your king also helps defend your own queen on d6, so the move wins material while keeping an important piece protected. How to find it: Use this simple checklist when you see queens near each other: first ask, 'Is my opponent’s queen hanging?' Then check whether taking it leaves any important piece of yours loose. Here, after Kxf6, your queen on d5 becomes defended by your king, so the trade works. Look for safe trades where you take a free valuable piece and do not forget to count attackers vs defenders before playing it.
